behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/LLM-DEP: A stubbed embedding client can always return the expected length, so it can hide real failures like model/version drift changing output dimensionality. The behavior explicitly depends on model output shape ("embedding vector produced ... has the correct dimensionality"), so minimum is Agentic. REAL-INFRA may also exist (HTTP), but System is lower than Agentic here because correctness depends on real model output. Rejected Workflow: behavior text covers one model step only, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run against the real embedding model used in production and the real index metadata. For fixed queries, assert returned embedding length equals the vector index dimension; fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: This can fail due to real vector DB behavior/configuration (metric mismatch, ANN/index settings, score semantics) that in-process fakes won’t reproduce. So minimum is System. Rejected Agentic: the behavior text is deterministic ordering logic ("ordered by descending cosine similarity score"), not model output quality/judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real vector DB instance configured like production, load a controlled fixture corpus and query vectors, execute real search calls, and assert returned scores are monotonic non-increasing and match cosine metric expectations.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: This behavior is explicitly about LLM judgment quality (nuanced intent affecting ordering). Deterministic mocks would trivially pass and cannot validate whether the model actually reorders appropriately. Rejected Workflow: only one model decision point is required (the re-ranker step), not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: For a benchmark set of nuanced queries with known candidate pools, call the real re-ranking LLM and compare pre/post order; require at least one meaningful reordering in expected cases across repeated runs with tolerance for non-determinism.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP/MOCK-HIDE: "No close matches" is a relevance-quality claim tied to real embedding behavior and similarity distribution; stubs can hide false positives/threshold calibration failures. Minimum is Agentic because correctness depends on real model-produced representations, even if assertion is deterministic. Rejected Workflow: behavior does not require multiple sequential model decisions; it is satisfied by validating the single-query relevance gate outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Using the real embedding model and real vector store, issue out-of-domain/no-match queries against a curated corpus and assert the API returns empty results under production thresholds (no low-score fallback items).

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP/REAL-INFRA: This is full pipeline latency across sequential steps (embedding model call + vector search + LLM re-rank + API path). The failure appears in cumulative, real, multi-step execution, so Workflow is the minimum boundary. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is explicitly end-to-end across multiple stages ("from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results"), not a single model step.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like load against the live end-to-end stack, with real embedding and re-ranking calls, and measure p50 latency over representative traffic; fail if median exceeds 2.0s.
